name: FPS Game Base
description: Complete FPS game base copied from UE5's official TP_FirstPerson template. C++ Character (camera, WASD, mouse look, jump), PlayerController (Enhanced Input, cursor hidden), GameMode, CameraManager (pitch limits). All controls are in C++ — do NOT attempt to recreate with Blueprint nodes.

requires.notes: After setup+build+launch, must call set_game_mode('BP_FirstPersonGameMode') per level. GlobalDefaultGameMode in DefaultEngine.ini is set but World Settings override takes priority.

character.notes: Camera attached to capsule at Z+64 (above capsule center at Z=96 from ground = 160 eye height). Original template uses skeletal mesh 'head' socket but that requires Mannequin mesh assets.

DefaultInput.ini: Full Enhanced Input settings from template

test_checklist: WASD moves relative to camera direction
Character stands at proper eye height (~160 units)
Gravity pulls character down after jumping
critical_lessons: NEVER recreate FPS controls with Blueprint nodes. Enhanced Input, cursor hiding, and movement all require C++ from the official template.
Camera must attach to CapsuleComponent at Z+64, NOT to FirstPersonMesh 'head' socket (requires Mannequin assets).
IMPLEMENT_GAME_MODULE (2 args), NOT IMPLEMENT_PRIMARY_GAME_MODULE (3 args) — project already has a primary module.
Must call set_game_mode per level after setup — GlobalDefaultGameMode alone is insufficient if World Settings has an override.
Build.cs variant paths must be stripped (we only copy base files, not Horror/Shooter variants).
